what_it_does: Records a verbatim primary-source exchange in which Tim states how the Company's written record must stay coherent through integration rather than versioning or instructions, and the assistant responds by unpacking the principle, naming an anti-pattern in TIM.md, proposing a source/synthesis split, and listing open architectural consequences.
observations: Tim's message is reproduced verbatim from TTS, with bracketed readability corrections and preserved errors.
Tim says dense messages are for eliciting a good response, not for the assistant to propose where or how to update structure.
When Tim does ask for changes, he wants option (a) plus a full map of every place the change relates to.
Tim rejects versioning because equal agents will treat their own recent edits as more important, causing regression and disharmony.
The exchange asserts that no human will ever read, write, review, or edit any of these files.
Tim discourages specifics and instructions in the doctrine, while encouraging independent thought and correction from agents.
The assistant identifies TIM.md's revision log, date stamps, and layer strata as an instance of the versioning anti-pattern.
The assistant proposes a clean separation: an immutable dated source layer and a timeless authorless synthesis layer.
Integration is framed as minimal and preserving, not a license to rewrite the body in the current agent's voice.
The response names unresolved problems: a coherence linter, the reflective map, integration protocol, and a tie-breaker for conflicting agent reads.
standouts: No human will ever read or write these files, so whatever is written becomes the sole record shaping every future agent.
The doctrine should be one coherent body maintained by integration, not an append-log, and should have no versioning.
No agent should be able to obey the record — only to understand from it.
The source/synthesis split is described as a CQRS-shaped knowledge architecture.
conventions: Uses exchange frontmatter with type, sequence, title, date, session, verbatim flag, note, produced list, and tags.
Tim's dictated message is presented in a blockquote with [bracketed] readability corrections.
The assistant's response is preserved verbatim under its own heading.
Ends with a 'What this produced' section and an 'Open at this exchange' section.
References other files using wiki-style and relative links.
concerns: The response admits TIM.md already violates the no-versioning principle but says it was left in place, creating a stated-vs-actual tension.
Several named mechanisms (coherence linter, reflective map, integration protocol, tie-breaker) are described but not codified in this file.
The phrase 'option (a)' is used without explanation inside this file.
Some sentences are long and nested, which may reduce parseability for future agents.
notes: A load-bearing governance exchange that defines how the Company's doctrine must be kept coherent, versionless, and instructive rather than instructional under zero human oversight.
questions: What does 'option (a)' refer to in this context?
What is 'Vi Memory' and how complete is the reflective map it holds?
What is the exact protocol for minimal-preserving integration?
How would a coherence linter detect drift without itself becoming another agent that overwrites peers?
What constitutes 'ground truth' for factual conflict arbitration?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file is presented as a verbatim primary-source exchange and it is one, while also recording the derived principles, README update, and open items.
apparent_themes: Coherence through integration rather than appending
Rejection of versioning and recency bias
Zero human oversight and equal-agent ecology
Guided commitment rather than proactive proposal
intention_signals: To establish a governance norm that the Company's doctrine must be maintained as a single timeless, authorless, integrated understanding, because future agents will rely entirely on the written record with no human correction.
novelty: Explicitly treats versioning as a recency-bias failure mode in a multi-agent system with no human review, and pairs it with a CQRS-style source/synthesis knowledge architecture where doctrine is timeless and evidence is immutable.
contribution: A verbatim articulation of the coherence-over-versioning principle, the source/synthesis split, the minimal-preserving-integration guardrail, and a named set of open design problems that any fused system would need to address.
summary_for_embedding: A verbatim exchange establishing that the Company's doctrine must be kept as a single coherent, versionless, instruction-free synthesis because no human will ever review it and thousands of equal agents will read it.
intention_for_embedding: To record the moment the no-versioning, integration-only, understanding-not-instructions governance principle was stated and interpreted.
